topic: Gemma 4 vs Llama 4 vs Qwen 3: Best Open-Source LLM for Developers 2026

key_points: Compares Llama 4, Qwen 3.5, DeepSeek V4, Gemma 4, and Mistral Medium 3.5
Ranks Maverick highest MMLU (85.5%) among open models
Recommends Gemma 4 for consumer hardware and Qwen 3.5 for coding workflows
Detailed 2026 comparison guide covering architecture, benchmarks, and licensing
Highlights Llama 4 Scout's 10M token context window as key differentiator
Emphasizes Apache 2.0 advantage for Gemma 4 and Qwen 3.5 over Llama 4 custom license
Updated benchmarks showing Gemma 4 31B at 80% on LiveCodeBench v6
Qwen 3.5 leads SWE-bench Verified at 72.4% — best for real-world coding
2026 open-source LLM landscape overview with all major players
DeepSeek V4 vs Llama 4 vs Qwen 3.5 vs Gemma 4 head-to-head
Notes that 2026 open-source models are now on par with proprietary models in many areas
Practical deployment guide: which open model to actually use
Inference speed: Qwen 3.5 27B ~35 tok/s, Gemma 4 31B ~25 tok/s on RTX 4090 Q4
Recommends Gemma 4 as default for single-GPU deployments

stats.fact: Gemma 4 31B scores 89.2% on AIME 2026, up from Gemma 3 27B's 20.8% — a 330% improvement
Gemma 4 31B LiveCodeBench v6: 80.0%; Codeforces ELO jumped from 110 (Gemma 3) to 2,150 — equivalent to an expert competitive programmer
Llama 4 Maverick: MMLU 85.5%, MMLU-Pro 80.5%, beating GPT-4o across multiple benchmarks
Llama 4 Scout features a 10 million token context window — the largest in any open-weight model as of 2026
Qwen3-235B-A22B: GPQA Diamond 77.2%, AIME '24 85.7% — strongest open-weight model for pure reasoning and math
Qwen 3.6-35B-A3B SWE-bench Verified: 73.4% — best practical coding benchmark measuring real GitHub issue resolution
Gemma 4 agentic tool-use τ2-bench (Retail): 86.4% — a 1,200% jump from Gemma 3's 6.6%
Llama 4 Scout (109B total, 17B active) is 2.1x cheaper per token than Llama 4 Maverick (400B total, 17B active)
Qwen 3 hybrid thinking mode lets developers toggle reasoning on/off per-request, saving tokens on simple tasks while enabling deep CoT on hard ones
Llama 4 custom license restricts apps with 700M+ monthly active users; Gemma 4 and Qwen 3 both use Apache 2.0 with zero usage restrictions

angles: Consumer hardware vs server-only: Gemma 4 31B runs on a single consumer GPU while Llama 4 requires data center hardware — for indie developers and small teams, this is the deciding factor
Hybrid thinking mode advantage: Qwen 3's toggle between fast and deep reasoning is uniquely practical for production apps where some queries need reasoning and others need speed
License risk for startups: Llama 4's 700M MAU cap is irrelevant today but could force an expensive migration at the worst possible moment of growth
Coding specialist vs generalist: Gemma 4 excels at competitive-style coding (Codeforces ELO 2,150) while Qwen 3 leads on real-world GitHub issue resolution (SWE-bench 73.4%)
Context window trade-offs: Llama 4 Scout's 10M token window dominates for massive codebase RAG; Qwen 3.6 Plus's 1M hits the sweet spot for most enterprise use cases
The multimodal catch-up: Both Gemma 4 and Llama 4 handle vision natively — open-source multimodal is now table stakes, not a differentiator
